_comment: Policy for the TemplatePromotionFromFeedback v1 feature. The Mac app reads triggerSignalWeights at capture time. The web admin Cloud Functions (runDiscriminator, promoteTemplateCandidate) read the rest at promotion time. Edit + git push + (relaunch / Cloud Function redeploy) ships changes with no Swift rebuild. See docs/TEMPLATE_PROMOTION_FROM_FEEDBACK.md.
_minConfidenceComment: Web admin filters the review queue at this threshold by default. Below this, the candidate is captured + screened but stays in 'low-confidence' view requiring an explicit admin opt-in to surface.
_triggerWeightsComment: Used by web admin queue ordering: weight × discriminator.confidence × (1 + endProduct.averageRating). Publish weighs higher because it's a stronger user-commitment signal (public listing on Community Hub) than a private deploy URL. v1 dropped looksGreat + rating triggers — those don't produce stable shareable URLs.
_directoryComment: Promoted templates land at mchatai-source/harness/templates/<subdir>/<slug>/. Per-type subdirs let TemplateCatalogService.resolve(prompt:, solutionType:) lookup find the right kind of seed. Renaming the subdirs is a content change here + a git mv — no Swift code change required.
_discriminatorProviderComment: Cloud Function picks this provider via its corresponding API key secret (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY). Currently defaulting to OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini) since the project's existing OPENAI_API_KEY billing is funded — Anthropic credits not yet topped up. Switch back to anthropic by editing this field; runDiscriminator picks up the new value on the next invocation, no Cloud Function redeploy needed.
_discriminatorModelComment: Models per provider for the discriminator call. UPGRADED 2026-04-30 from gpt-4o-mini to gpt-4o after a QA-flywheel run on Minesweeper showed mini systematically false-declined classic-genre miniApps by reasoning from world knowledge ('Minesweeper exists in many places') instead of from EXISTING_TEMPLATES_BY_CATEGORY. gpt-4o correctly promotes the same artifact with the same prompt. Cost: ~$0.04/run vs ~$0.001/run for mini — 40x more, but still ~$1 per 25 runs, which is fine for an admin-screened queue. Drop back to mini only if a future prompt structure forces world-knowledge reasoning out reliably.
_autoFilterComment: If a category already has N templates, any new candidate in it must beat 0.85 confidence to surface in the default queue view. Prevents catalog bloat in popular categories. Empty map = no auto-filter. Web admin can override per-row.
_screeningCapComment: When the admin opens the queue (or a 'Screen all' tunnel command fires), screen at most this many unscreened candidates per call. Prevents accidentally burning a large API budget on an aged backlog.
